hypothesis: Incorporating molecular charge as a model input feature—which the authors identify as a planned future capability—will disproportionately improve MD simulation stability and force prediction accuracy for drug-like molecules containing ionizable groups (amines, carboxylic acids, phosphates) compared to neutral organic molecules, such that a charge-conditioned MACE model trained on the full SPICE2 dataset including the 142,647 currently excluded non-neutral structures will reduce force MAE by more than 30% on solvated amino acid subsets relative to the charge-agnostic pre-trained models presented here, while maintaining or improving simulation stability on the existing benchmark systems 1UAO and 1ABT.
